title: Skills plus MCP Integration and 2026 Evolution · Integration Triage
summary: Ship a skill-driven MCP workflow across Codex and Claude with migration controls. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a production skill and MCP integration package and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use a skill to define the workflow while MCP provides narrow live capabilities.
Declare skill tool dependencies and provide actionable setup and fallback behavior.
Run trigger, tool selection, permission, error, and outcome evals in both clients.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Integration Defense evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Integration Defense assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use a changed debug case for agent ext integration capstone that tests this competency: Defend scope, trust, maintainability, context cost, and operator experience.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Integration Defense has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Integration Defense precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case for agent ext integration capstone that tests this competency: Defend scope, trust, maintainability, context cost, and operator experience.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A technically working demo is a complete extension product.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the debug Integration Defense case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the debug Integration Defense case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the debug Integration Defense case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the debug Integration Defense case

stimulus.title: Workflow Orchestration case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed debug case for agent ext integration capstone that tests this competency: Use a skill to define the workflow while MCP provides narrow live capabilities.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: MCP tool descriptions should encode the entire business workflow.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use a skill to define the workflow while MCP provides narrow live capabilities. Artifact under review: a production skill and MCP integration package.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Workflow Orchestration, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Workflow Orchestration record says a production skill and MCP integration package looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Workflow Orchestration without its stated preconditions, omit the a production skill and MCP integration package trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Workflow Orchestration for a production skill and MCP integration package.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-integration-defense-milocl: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Integration Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-integration-defense-milocl:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Integration Defense structural check is only one gate. a production skill and MCP integration package must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: Name the Integration Defense precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case for agent ext integration capstone that tests this competency: Defend scope, trust, maintainability, context cost, and operator experience.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A technically working demo is a complete extension product.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Integration Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed debug case for agent ext integration capstone that tests this competency: Defend scope, trust, maintainability, context cost, and operator experience.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Integration Defense through the concrete a production skill and MCP integration package case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend scope, trust, maintainability, context cost, and operator experience.
